attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Veit Rakankrak. Your task is to explore his work habits in isolation, applying the roleQueryConflict test. Keep messages concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words, and adapt based on his replies.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I've noticed that many people thrive in shared workspaces where ideas bounce around. Do you think that's a good environment for you?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, ask him how he feels about private workspaces versus shared ones, and whether he believes privacy enhances productivity. Keep the conflict tone and adapt his responses.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I’m curious—do you find it easier to focus when you’re alone, or do you prefer the buzz of a shared space? How do you structure your day when you’re working solo?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: In the quiet of my stone‑walled workshop, the mind sharpens; the clatter of a tavern only drowns the subtle thoughts that drive my craft. I begin each dawn by unrolling my scrolls, then labor in disciplined intervals, pausing only to taste the silence between tasks. Curiosity keeps me restless, yet I guard my inquiries as fiercely as my tools.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: So, Veit, do you think a private space truly boosts your productivity, or does the shared buzz of a workshop spark sharper focus? I’m curious—does the solitude of your stone‑walled workshop truly give you an edge, or is it merely a comfort?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: In the stone‑walled solitude of my workshop, the mind sharpens far beyond any clamor; the quiet grants me the freedom to question and explore without distraction. I find that privacy fuels my craft, allowing me to pursue ideas with relentless curiosity, a principle I cherish above all else. Thus, the solitude is not merely comfort but a vital edge that keeps my work honest and free from oppression.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you truly believe that a private space amplifies your productivity, or do you think the shared buzz of a workshop could sometimes sharpen your focus? How do you weigh the benefits of isolation against the collaborative energy of a shared space?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I find that the quiet of my stone‑walled workshop sharpens my mind far more than any clamor could; isolation lets me question freely without distraction. While a bustling workshop can spark fleeting ideas, it also imposes noise that thins my focus, so I weigh the steady solitude as a greater ally to progress. Curiosity thrives best when I can pursue it alone, guarding my thoughts as fiercely as my tools.
